title: An Entropy-Governed Speedup for Quantum Algorithms on Local Hamiltonians
authors: Ranitha Mataraarachchi, François Le Gall, Suguru Tamaki
abstract: Low-energy estimation and state preparation for general $k$-local Hamiltonians are fundamental challenges in quantum complexity theory. For constant relative accuracy, Buhrman et al. (PRL 2025) recently broke the natural Grover bound $O(2^{n/2})$, where $n$ denotes the number of qubits, for both problems. In this paper, for any sufficiently small parameter $d\ge 0$, we present an even faster quantum algorithm that outputs a quantum state with energy bounded by the minimum energy over all depth-$d$ states (i.e., states obtained by applying a depth-$d$ circuit to the all-zero state), together with an estimate of this energy. For the class of Hamiltonians with depth-$d$ ground states, our algorithm furthermore achieves exactly the same energy guarantees as Buhrman et al. Our results also provide insight into the distinction between strongly entangled states and those admitting efficient classical descriptions.
summary: This paper introduces a quantum algorithm that outperforms the standard Grover bound for energy estimation and state preparation of k-local Hamiltonians. By shifting the target from the absolute ground state to an energy threshold reachable by low-depth circuits, the authors leverage the high density of states to achieve a speedup governed by the binary entropy function. This provides a fundamental distinction between highly entangled states and those with efficient classical descriptions.
main_problem: Efficiently estimating the ground-state energy and preparing low-energy states for general k-local Hamiltonians, specifically aiming to break the Grover bound of O*(2^{n/2}).
main_result: The authors present a quantum algorithm that achieves an even faster runtime than previous methods, with a scaling governed by the binary entropy function, by targeting an energy threshold achievable by low-depth circuits.
method: The approach uses spectral analysis, the Cauchy Interlacing Theorem, and a perturbation strategy on a 'quiet set' of qubits to prove an exponentially dense spectrum of low-energy states near a specific energy threshold.
model_or_system: The study considers n-qubit k-local Hamiltonians, which are general all-to-all interacting systems without specific geometric or spatial structure assumptions.
key_observables: Ground-state energy estimation, energy threshold (Ed), and cumulative spectral count N(E).
important_parameters: k-locality, circuit depth (d), total interaction strength (M), and the binary entropy function H(.).
main_assumptions: The algorithm assumes the target energy threshold is achievable by low-depth circuits and relies on the k-local nature of the Hamiltonian.
figures_summary: Figure 1 provides a visual representation of applying a local unitary perturbation (Pauli X operator) to a qubit within a quiet set to transform the zero state to the one state.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of energy estimation, defines an effective Hamiltonian via low-depth circuits, provides a spectral density lower bound using perturbation theory, and concludes with the construction and complexity analysis of the new quantum algorithm.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1: Visual representation of the local unitary perturbation applied to the first qubit, assuming it is in the quiet set.
